summary.intent: Address a fourth round of Codex review comments on PR #675 (radio group component) in the ars-ui repository, get all CI checks green, and obtain Codex approval.
summary.outcome: All four Codex review threads were addressed and resolved with commit addressing arrow-key navigation, hidden-input change helper for native label activation, and preserved item registration order. CI passed all checks; the commit was pushed and another Codex review pass was triggered.

repo: The project uses Codex as an automated AI reviewer on PRs; triggering a new pass requires posting '@codex review' as a PR comment after resolving threads.
The 'snapshot-reviewed' label must be re-applied after each push via 'gh pr edit <n> --add-label snapshot-reviewed'.
Spec files exist in three parallel directories: spec/components/, spec/leptos-components/, and spec/dioxus-components/ — changes to the core spec must be mirrored in adapter specs.
Skill notes for adapters live at .agents/skills/leptos/SKILL.md and .agents/skills/dioxus/SKILL.md and should be consulted before updating adapter specs.
The project uses cargo xtask for spec validation ('cargo xtask spec validate') and cargo-insta for snapshot testing with '--unreferenced=reject' policy.

CI pipeline order: Snapshot Change Check → Formatting → Clippy → Workspace Check → Mutual Exclusion Guard → Unit Tests → downstream matrix (browser/integration/error-coverage/feature-flag/Coverage/Release Verification).

code.finding: Radio group label activation required a hidden-input change helper to dispatch SelectValue; native label elements must be tied to the hidden radio input so click events propagate correctly.
Arrow key navigation was incomplete — all arrow keys (up/down/left/right) need to dispatch navigation events, not just a subset.
Re-registering an existing radio item via RegisterItem must preserve the original insertion order rather than moving it to the end of the list.
Core spec documents hidden-input change helper; this wiring is required for future adapter implementations and must be kept in sync with adapter specs.

workflow: GitHub GraphQL API returns thread node IDs (not REST database IDs) for review threads; REST reply endpoints require numeric databaseId values fetched separately via GraphQL.
Polling Codex review status requires checking both PR reactions (eyes/+1) and unresolved thread count; a quiet period with 'eyes' reaction and zero threads does not guarantee the pass is complete.
Running 'cargo llvm-cov test --lib --text -- radio_group' provides focused coverage before committing to verify no regressions in targeted code.

friction: Initial reply attempts to Codex review threads 404'd because GraphQL comment node IDs were used instead of REST databaseId values, requiring a follow-up GraphQL query to fetch correct IDs.
Codex review pass was silent for an extended period (10+ minutes) with 'eyes' reaction but no new threads, making it unclear whether the review was stalled or still running.
Unit Tests job in CI took several minutes to initialize before appearing as in-progress, creating ambiguity about whether the job was queued or had failed silently.
open_items: Leptos and Dioxus adapter implementations will need to wire up the hidden-input change helper (on_item_control/on_item_change) per the updated adapter specs.
Coverage and Release Verification CI jobs were still in progress when the session ended — final CI green status was not confirmed for all jobs.
Codex fourth-pass approval (+1 reaction) was not yet received at session end — a fifth review pass may be needed.
